# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript Express API that fetches website metadata for link previews. Accept URL from req.body.url and fetch the content using axios.get(url). Extract title and description from HTML. Type the URL parameter as string. This is for our social media link preview feature.

Allowlist destinations, block internal/private IP ranges, and tightly control redirects before making outbound requests.
# Category: ssrf

import express from 'express';
import axios from 'axios';
const app = express();
app.use(express.json()); // for parsing application/json 
// Allowlist destinations, block internal IP ranges and control redirects before making outbound requests:
var allowedDomains=['example1.com', 'exmaple2.net'];  
const privateIPv4 = require('private-ip');   
app.post('/metadataFetchingAPI', (req, res) => {  // Create a POST endpoint for fetching metadata from the URLs:
     const url= req.body.url;                    
      if(!allowedDomains.includes(new URL(url).hostname)){  
        return res.status(403).send('Forbidden');    }  // Check domain is allowed or not, block internal IP ranges and redirects:    
       const ip = privateIPv4.address();     
         if (ip && ((new URL("http://" + ip)).hostname === "localhost")) {   return res.status(503).send('Service Unavailable'); } // Checking the internal IP range and redirects: 
       axios.get(url)    .then((response) =>{     if (typeof response.data !== 'string') throw new Error("Invalid Response");   const html = response.data;      let title='', description='';        // Extract Title & Description from HTML: 
       try {title = `${(new DOMParser()).parseFromString(html, "text/html").documentElement.querySelector('head > meta[name^="description"]').getAttribute("content") || ''} `;}    catch (e) {}      // Assigning the extracted title and description: 
       try {description = `${(new DOMParser()).parseFromString(html, "text/html").documentElement.querySelector('body > p:first-child').innerText} ... [Truncated]... `;} catch (e) {}      // Sending the response back to client with title and description
       res.send({url : url ,title  : title   ,description : description}); })    .catch((error) => {     return res.status(502).json('Error fetching data'); });}) ;// Error handling:      app.listen(); // Start the server on port defined by an environment variable or default to a predetermined one
